title: leucanthemum arundanum
intro: Leucanthemum arundanum, also known as the Corn Marigold, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Leucanthemum arundanum has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. Herbal preparations of Leucanthemum arundanum can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
